es.example: Se quedó en casa todo el día.

it.meaning: To stay; to be left over.
it.example: È rimasto a casa tutto il giorno.

bridge.note: Spanish has one perfect auxiliary (haber) and it never varies. Italian chooses between essere and avere, and rimanere takes essere — so the participle has to agree, which Spanish never asks of you.

interference.es_to_it: Spanish speakers produce 'ha rimasto' by analogy with 'ha quedado'. Rimanere takes essere: è rimasto / è rimasta.
interference.it_to_es: Italians over-apply agreement and say 'se ha quedada'. Spanish participles after haber never agree: se ha quedado.

review.notes: Renumbered from 0007 on import of the study-thread bank (id collision). Authored as a schema demo; sources not human-verified; reviewer sign-off pending. cue: How do you say you're staying put?
